w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Heat olive oil in a skillet; sauté garlic until fragrant.
Add chorizo, cook until lightly crisp.
season with paprika, salt, and pepper.
Cook until shrimp are opaque and tender.
Finish with lemon juice and parsley.
• Serve immediately for best texture; can hold warm ≤165°F briefly.
Dredge veal in flour, dip in eggs, coat with breadcrumbs and Parmesan.
Pan-fry in olive oil until golden and cooked through.
Season with salt & pepper, serve with lemon wedges.
Roast monkfish fillets in olive oil until opaque.
Sauté garlic and onion until soft, add tomatoes, saffron, and
Blend lightly for broth consistency; season with salt and pepper.
• Broth holds refrigerated 2–3 days; monkfish best served immediately.
Sauté shallots and garlic in olive oil; add mushrooms and thyme.
Serve mushroom ragout over polenta cakes.
• Ragout can hold ≤165°F; polenta best served freshly grilled.
Blanch broccoli rabe in salted water 2–3 minutes; shock in ice water.
Sauté garlic and red pepper flakes in olive oil.
Toss broccoli rabe with oil and season.
Toss tomatoes with olive oil, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Roast at 400°F until soft and slightly caramelized.
• Serve warm or at room temperature.
Sauté onion in olive oil until translucent.
Gradually add stock, stirring constantly, until rice is creamy and tender.
Finish with butter, Parmesan, and lemon zest; adjust seasoning.
Mix dry ingredients; combine with wet ingredients until smooth.
Pour into pans; bake until golden and set.
Cool slightly; optionally drizzle with glaze.
• Store covered at room temperature or
Baked Escargot, Garlic Parsley Butter (Hot Appetizer, 8 oz)
Combine butter, garlic, parsley, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Place escargot in individual shells or mini dishes; top with garlic-parsley butter.
Bake until butter is bubbling and fragrant, ~10–12 minutes.
• Serve immediately for best flavor; can hold warm ≤165°F briefly.
Sear beef on all sides; brush with Dijon mustard.
Sauté mushrooms and shallots in butter until moisture evaporates; cool.
Wrap beef and mushroom mixture in puff
Brush with egg wash; bake at 400°F until golden, internal ~130°F.
• Hold briefly warm ≤165°F; best served immediately.
Brown chicken in butter and olive oil; remove from pan.
Sauté onions and carrots; deglaze with cider and stock.
Return chicken to pan; simmer until fully cooked, ~30–35 min.
• Hold ≤165°F; sauce can be held separately.
Mix ricotta, Parmesan, garlic, salt, and pepper.
place in baking dish with marinara.
Toss zucchini with olive oil, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Roast at 400°F until tender and lightly browned.
Sauté onions in butter until lightly browned.
Add sugar and stock; simmer until onions are glazed.
Layer potato slices in buttered pans; season with salt, pepper, and thyme.
Bake at 400°F until golden and crisp on top, tender inside.
Whip eggs with sugar until pale and fluffy.
Fold chocolate into eggs; gently fold in whipped cream and vanilla.
Smoked Gouda Stuffed Mushrooms (Hot Appetizer, 8 oz)
Mix stems with Gouda, breadcrumbs, parsley.
Sear venison loins in olive oil; grill to desired doneness.
: sauté juniper berries, deglaze with wine, add stock, reduce by half.
Coat fillets with seasoning; sear in hot pan with oil until blackened and cooked through.
• Best served immediately; hold briefly warm ≤165°F.
Layer sweet potatoes, kale, garlic, cream, and cheese in baking dish.
Bake at 375°F until tender and golden.
Toss beets with oil, salt, and pepper; roast at 400°F until tender.
Roll out dough; spread almond cream.
Layer pear slices; fold edges to form crostata.
Brush with butter and bake at 375°F until golden.
• Hold at room temperature; serve sliced.
Steam bao buns until warm and soft.
Fill each bun with pork belly, cucumber,
drizzle with hoisin and sesame oil.
Brown oxtail in olive oil; remove.
Sauté aromatics; add wine and stock.
Add oxtail and star anise; braise at 325°F until tender, ~3–4 hours.
• Hold ≤165°F; sauce can be held separately.
Seared Scallopine of Turkey, Marsala Cream
Sear turkey scallopine in olive oil and butter until golden.
Deglaze with Marsala; reduce by half.
Add cream; return turkey, coat in sauce.
Toss butter and miso to create miso butter.
Sauté vegetables lightly; toss with noodles and miso butter.
Sauté or blanch peas; season lightly with oil and salt.
Toss mushrooms with oil, salt, and pepper; roast at 400°F until browned.
Simmer rice in milk until tender.
Add coconut milk, sugar, vanilla; cook gently until creamy.
Heat cream, sugar, and sesame paste; stir until smooth.
Sauté lobster with butter and garlic.
Mix bone marrow with butter; serve on top of steak.
Ingredients (Use market fish of choice, 6 oz portions)
Sear fish in olive oil until cooked through.
Make a roux with butter and flour; add milk to make béchamel.
Fold in spinach, cheese, egg yolks, and seasoning.
Whip egg whites; fold gently into mixture.
Bake at 375°F until puffed and golden.
• Serve immediately; soufflé will deflate if held too long.
Toss cauliflower with oil, salt, pepper; roast at 400°F until golden.
Blanch; sauté lightly with butter and salt.
Boil potatoes; mash with butter and cream.
Fold in hazelnuts; bake at 350°F until set.
• Hold at room temperature or refrigerated; serve sliced.
Spicy Calamari, Citrus Aioli (Hot Appetizer, 8 oz)
Sauté calamari with oil, garlic, red pepper flakes until cooked.
Serve with citrus aioli (mix mayo with lemon juice).
Brown lamb shanks in oil; remove.
Sauté aromatics; add wine and stock.
Return lamb; braise at 325°F until tender, ~3–4 hrs.
Sauté onions and mushrooms; mix with cooked rice and butter.
Stuff squash; finish in oven at 375°F until heated.
Sauté celeriac in butter; add stock and thyme.
Toss mushrooms with oil, salt, pepper; roast at 400°F until golden.
finish with garlic, butter, and season.
Roll dough; bake blind until lightly golden.
Whisk eggs and milk; fold in flour, melted butter, salt, and chives.
Pour batter into greased muffin tins or popover molds; bake at 400°F until puffed and golden.
• Serve immediately; popovers deflate if held too long.
Rub tenderloin with olive oil, garlic, rosemary, salt, and pepper.
Roast at 425°F to desired doneness (~130–135°F internal for medium rare).
slice just before service for best texture.
Mix maple syrup, mustard, and olive oil to make glaze.
Sauté leeks in butter until soft; cool slightly.
Whisk eggs, milk, cream, salt, and pepper.
Toss bread, leeks, and cheese; pour custard over.
Bake at 375°F until set and golden.
• Serve warm ≤165°F; can be reheated gently.
Blanch peas; toss with butter and salt.
Toss parsnips with oil, salt, and pepper; roast at 400°F until caramelized.
Cook polenta in water or stock until tender.
Stir in butter, cream, sundried tomatoes, and
Whisk eggs, milk, cream, sugar, bourbon, and vanilla.
Toss with bread cubes; bake at 350°F until golden and set.
• Serve warm or at room temperature.
